title: Staying safe and knowing your rights :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Staying safe and knowing your rights
If you move to New Zealand, it is important to keep yourself and your family safe, and to know what your rights are.
- Your rights in New Zealand New Zealand has a range of laws intended to protect your human rights and other legal rights.

## Your rights in New Zealand
New Zealand has a range of laws intended to protect your human rights and other legal rights.
- Keeping yourself safe in New Zealand Although New Zealand is a relatively safe country to live in, it is important to know how to protect yourself, your family and your property.

## Keeping yourself safe in New Zealand
Although New Zealand is a relatively safe country to live in, it is important to know how to protect yourself, your family and your property.
- Police, emergency services and natural disasters New Zealand's police, fire and ambulance services can help you in an emergency, and Civil Defence can help in a flood, earthquake, tsumani or other natural disaster.

## Police, emergency services and natural disasters
New Zealand's police, fire and ambulance services can help you in an emergency, and Civil Defence can help in a flood, earthquake, tsumani or other natural disaster.
- Register interest in working in New Zealand Learn more about protecting yourself when searching for jobs and your rights while working in New Zealand.

## Register interest in working in New Zealand
Learn more about protecting yourself when searching for jobs and your rights while working in New Zealand.
